reviews.review_description: The whole game is amazing,  if your looking for a Narrative adventure , this is the game for you great story

This is a surprisingly good game. It really capture the world of the foundation novels. It is more an adventure game than an action one, where you have a lot of conversations, and your joices actually matters! I really do enjoy this game.

The story has good parts over shadowed by  inconsistency and parts that don't fit or make sense for the characters. Most of the voice acting was good though. Performance is fine but with graphics that look a gen or two back, it had better be. Combat meh, so passable since it's not an action game. Parts of the game, it's unclear what the game wants you to interact with or how to interact with it. Puzzle feel more like filler at times. I did get, some, enjoyment playing it but would have been disappointed if i didn't get it on sale for how lacking in general it was.
Tell me, there's another one coming out.This was amazing
Loving everything about it so far, just wish it had Achievements...

I think it’s worth it the story is really good the fighting is ok it just feels like it’s missing a leveling up system like there are some side quest but you don’t get anything from them as far is I could see that’s my ownly problem

So let's start with positive: I liked the voice acting the art style has a charms and overall story was interesting
But it's kind of buggy low resolution art style putting me off the choices are like telltale style...very limited the word interaction are okay the combat I would say is very basic just as the hacking mini game and the end kind of disappointed me I expected to see the foundation but the game ended there so overall the game have a lot of potential and I hope they will work on it in the meanwhile it's an okay game the length of gameplay it's ok

I enjoyed the story, settings, and characters, and the voice acting was excellent. The combat was just right for me, i.e. not too much of it, and not too hard. 😅 The controls weren't always intuitive to me, so I think I missed out on some things, and in a few places I struggled for a while before I figured out how to progress. Also, be aware that some of your choices can have unintended consequences later on, which you can't avoid because you can only go back to your last checkpoint. But overall, I enjoyed it very much. 😊👍

reviews.review_title: A fun choose your own adventure
reviews.review_description: It's a fun game, where your decisions affect the outcome. I wish they graphics were a bit better on Quest. They don't really match the trailer in the Quest store, but the game itself is pretty fun, and it's a good story.
I really enjoyed playing this. Lots of hours of entertainment, and some very good voice acting from the MPC. I do hope, that you are making more games like this?

Great story based game. Graphics are fine, action sequences are skippable which is odd, better if this was an option rather than a prompt each time you have to climb. Combat is basic but passable to break things up but the controls and instructions is where it falls down hard. You make choices by tapping icons in-front of you but it’s just too easy for your hands to already be there and trigger them without knowing what you chose. Simple fix  would be to allow button clicks in the settings. Perhaps the biggest issue are the instructions where it will be unclear or just wrong leading to confusion. Eg there’s a part where it says insert a keycard and then insert some parts but you don’t get the keycard till you insert the parts. Its like this throughout which means that you spend ages wasting time and it gets tedious. All these issues could be fixed easily and would make the game much more enjoyable. Could easily be a 4 or 5 star game. Shame

Really fun with a good interactive story. Even though I’m not great shooting a gun, there wasn’t any battle that I couldn’t eventually get past (might fail the mission 20 times, but oh well, I got to try again ;) Great game and story. Get it!

This is a sort of interactive story based on the Foundation novels.  But it also contains action sequences with shooting, climbing, and minor puzzles.

I'm enjoying it. I've read the novels so the theme is interesting to me.

This is almost more of an interactive story than a game. The bulk of this “game” is dialogue.  There isn’t much adventure or exploration.  Chapter tasks are pretty simple.  I kept rotating while using ￼￼my abilities which entails pushing down on the right thumbstick so that’s great when you’re getting shot at. I liked when i could climb something since I got to do something. The story is fine but I couldn’t focus on who was with what group and who was good or bad etc cuz I just wanted it to be over.

Journey to Foundation is a good space story adventure with some nice branching choices. Little surprises here and there. Voice Acting is on point unlike some other titles. This is like a slow paced Mass Effect in VR with more in Story than combat. Worth a go.
Once I started playing I could not stop. Kept wanting to see the hat happens next. It was a thrill.

reviews.review_title: The bones of a good game
reviews.review_description:
I really like the idea of this game, the voice acting is solid, the powers you get are fun to use, the world building is pretty good, the story is interesting, but there are a lot of frustrating parts too. Many puzzles are not clear what you're supposed to do, and even when they explicitly tell you, the environment seems set up against you.

Put in the engine cores where? Okay I need a key card, where is it? Oh this NPC gives it to me after I talk to them a second time. Dial this thing into the right frequency? Okay, I feel like these two dials vibrate in this specific setting, but not in another setting, but nothing's happening after several minutes. Oh there's this whole hologram that shows exactly what you're supposed to be doing that nobody told me to look at.

There are times when this game is a 5*, when everything hits and you're figuring out secrets it feels like you're on an away mission in star trek. Then there are times when the game just keeps getting in it's own way, and it's a 3*, which is why I'm giving it a 4*.

I really want to see this studio take what they've learned from this and make another game, but it sounds like that might not be in the cards 💔

I liked this game enough to play it a couple of times through, to see how the outcomes would change (it was worthwhile to see two pretty different endings, both of which were good). First playthrough was as an Empire Agent who slowly gets converted to be on the side of the Foundation. The second was as an Empire loyalist with a somewhat evil streak (even spaced a prisoner after I told him he could go free!). Anyway, the game is like being in a play, where you get to read scripted lines, but can also influence some aspects of the story being told. There is a lot of world-building that went into this relatively short game (and I liked the length of it), with a wealth of information in the Encyclopedia. I wish they had used a system like Dyschronia: Chronos Alternate used, where you could quickly see which entries were new after each chapter and get to them faster, though. I lost interest in picking through the tree over time, but it was still interesting to read a lot of the entries. Don't expect too much excitement or challenge in the battles, but if you again approach them like you're an actor in a play, then you can have fun with them.  They didn't try to make the combat too hard, probably so that no one would get stuck due to poor shooting/survival skills. At least it breaks up the story a bit and allows you to do something else besides the dialogue interactions. The main thing I would have asked for if the game were still being maintained (RIP Archiact :(  ) would be the ability to go back to specific chapters at any time to change choices, without having to replay the entire game, but on the other hand, I did enjoy the second playthrough as well, and was more relaxed and into it, since I knew how to do everything. I didn't care for the puzzles, which were fun only a couple of times and didn't make sense as a requirement for "hacking". Overall, I recommend playing this for the story. Very nice to feel like part of the Foundation world for a while.

Good story, stays faithful to the books, though very short. Almost as short as a demo for a AAA game, and very buggy. Game-breaking bugs include things like clipping off the map (very disorienting in VR!), enemies stuck inside walls, required items falling through scenery when dropped, and scripted events failing to trigger. Each bug breaks the game and requires the user to restart the level and hope it doesn't happen again. I'd give it three stars if it didn't have the bugs. And I'd add two more if it were longer than a demo. Padding duration with excessively verbose dialog doesn't count. Don't pay full price.

"Journey to Foundation was a rather pleasant surprise. Offering an epic sci-fi quest in the span of around seven hours, I found myself gripped until the very end. The game’s heavy focus on conversations rather than action may not appeal to everyone, but this is quite frankly one of the best Sci-Fi games that I’ve had the pleasure of playing. I can only hope that we see a follow up further down the line."
Full review over at The Elite Institute

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your in-depth review, MYT! We're thrilled to hear you say our game is one of the best Sci-Fi games you've played!

reviews.review_description:
Actually kind of loved this but it’s not without flaws. 

It’s a little buggy, a few times events didn’t trigger for me or bad guys got stuck in walls or doors. It’s a little simple in terms of environments, scripting, interactivity etc. The ending pretty much sucks and screams “chapter 2 : coming soon” (but ok guys seriously when?! I need more). Looking out of windows on the ships you see a flat skybox. Would’ve been nice if the planets and stars had some depth. Would’ve been nice to fly the ship. 

Also playing seated isn’t great on this. The way menus and conversation selection works makes it really easy to hit the wrong thing (even before the options are fully visible) if your hands are in front of you at all. 

In many ways this feels like a ps2 era fps - it’s quite simple in many ways compared to modern AAA games but it’s got some heart and creativity. It feels fairly unique. The story is nice and pretty meaty as far as vr games go. Characters are great. It looks pretty good. Plays well. My review sounds harsh but I pretty much played this whole thing over a couple of days and loved the whole thing. 

Closest comparison I can think of is Hubris. I much preferred this.

I like the general idea of a story-based experience with a bit of classic vr injections here and there (shooting, climbing, puzzling), but in this particular case I personally didn't like neither the writing (esp the modern audiences part) nor the vr part (which is rather junky).

developer_response.body:
Hi there, thank you for taking the time to share your experience with Journey to Foundation. We're glad to hear you enjoyed the story-based experience and VR interactions.

We're sorry to hear you didn't enjoy other aspects of the game. If you run into any issues or bugs, please let us know by visiting support.journeytofoundation.com so we can investigate.

All the best,
The Archiact Team

reviews.review_description:
Journey to Foundation is a game that makes me sad and it makes me sad the same way the original mass effect made me sad and that's because just like the original mass effect this game was genuine greatness in its bones that is completely undermined by one of the most broken and buggy experiences of my life. 

The story, voice acting and graphics are amazing, the world building and freedom of choice are also amazing. But where it begins to fall apart is the combat which is the most boring, basic and broken combat of any game I have ever played, if the developers fixed the combat this game would be amazing but as is its a completely mediocre experience that not even the amazing story can save. 

But hands down the worst thing about this game is the gesture system, I cannot even tell you how many times this system bugged out, I failed so many times that I literally had my girl take a video of me playing just so I could watch a guide and compare exactly what I was doing wrong. But I wasn't doing anything wrong, either its just incredibly poorly implemented or the guest tracking just cannot handle it but either way the developers would have noticed this in testing and then they still decided to not only put it in the final game but to have major story repercussions tied to performing these gestures, absolutely ridiculous.  

Developers if you are reading this please I am begging you to take the time to fix this game because if these problems were ironed out it would be by far the best game on the quest platform, there are the bones of something genuinely amazing here. 

3/5

developer_response.body:
Hi there, thanks very much for taking the time to share your review of Journey to Foundation.

Could you please create a ticket at support.journeytofoundation.com with more information about the combat issues you're experiencing? We'd like to look into this, as we haven't had any other reports about the combat not working properly.

We'd also like to hear about which gestures you're having trouble with, so we can investigate further and try to help you out!

We did release a patch last year after we got player feedback about gestures being difficult to register, making it so that gestures registered more easily, so we'd like to investigate these areas of friction.

Again, please create a ticket at support.journeytofoundation.com with more information about the issues you're experiencing so we can investigate and ensure you have the intended experience! Thank you.

reviews.review_description: Everything is very much scripted and on rails. There isn’t much freedom to do anything differently. There are lots of slow, scripted events during which you can't move or do anything but watch/listen. Pressing a button takes 2 whole seconds. The story, gameplay, graphics, sound, and speech are all just mediocre. Control and movement are glitchy. If I have more patience and no other games to play, this game is worth playing.

IF YOU PLAYED KOTOR/SWTOR OR MASS EFFECT YOU MIGHT LOVE THIS GAME

This game is nearly everything I want in a VR interactive story, from the having to mimic enemy gestures during spy missions to your choices having actual effects on the storyline it's just a great experience. A lot id add to the game but for the price its playtime and replayability are worth it.

I don't have anything bad to say about the game besides it felt like the opposite of a sausage fest. 
However there's a lot of things that could've been improved and made more detailed.

For example the exploration of maps. I liked talking to NPCs and reading holoballs to learn lord but I feel like there could've been more done, something dumb like having mini games that relate to the planet you're on's inhabitants. Also doing these elaborate hacks on these big lockers or chests just.for a tiny ball to come out is a waste of map space, the balls should've just had some way you had to hack them to retrieve their information or the places they were hidden weren't so overly sized, weird issue to take up but if you open up your first crate and get disappointed like I did then you should understand 

Speaking of mini games I was sorta disappointed when I didn't get a chance to drive the ship. Especially when Kaldon asks if you've ever driven and you respond "only in simulations" it really made me think we were gonna get in the pilots seat

The modular gun is a nice concept but just having to choose between 3 different weapons gives people variety based off what they wanna use in combat it's just not that important because the shootouts in the game usually gets dominated by your mentalic abilities alone. More ways to modify your gun would be great but I feel like it's too much even as it is with the minimal amount of combat your encounter.

The mentalics are really cool and after my first playthrough I really wanna learn more about them (looking into the tv show and books(hopefully we get more additions to this franchise cause games like this are so awesome) and even with my limited information of them I feel like they werent treated right in the game. The way you read minds or influence people is fine and cool, I sorta wish there was a punishment system for failing to do these correctly but what I felt like was missing is a skilltree or just a better focus on our powers (really praying we get a sequel that evolves Wards abilities or a whole new mc and perspective of the universe together). The mentalics you use out of combat are fine like I said, but during fights it's so boring just sticking your hand out and slowly draining life, the only other strategy you can use with it in combat is tapping a shield guy with so he exposed his head for a second. A skilltree with a interface like in cyberpunk where you can choose different types of hacks on your enemies that change their actions or just straight up do damage. 

Even if these seem like me disliking the game I can't stress enough how much I love it, I just felt like there's a lot more that couldve been done to make it even more epic. 

There is one thing I hate about this game actually, Im in love with the story but we have no word if Wards adventure will continue. If we do get a sequel I beg it's mostly focused on Foundation and Ward training her mentalic abilities. Even a brand new story like would be great, wouldn't even be mad if the mc had no powers, that gives us a chance to see what it's like being on the receiving end of a mentalics manipulation. I just really hope we get more of this universe told through this format. This game reminds me so much of Mass Effect and Kotor/Swtor, but all told through the lens of VR. PLEASE support these developers so hopefully this series continues and lodges itself up there with those OG RPG games

developer_response.body:
Hi QuietOne! Thank you so much for your detailed review of Journey to Foundation. We're thrilled to hear that our game had so much that you enjoy in a VR game!

We're sorry to hear you were disappointed with some other aspects of the game, and appreciate all of your feedback and suggestions.

We're glad how much you enjoyed the story, and appreciate your kind words!

reviews.review_description: Just finished Journey to foundation. A good story driven game with a lot of dialogue. I appreciate the fact that your choices dictate how things will turn out through the game. I thought the combat was a bit uninspiring and felt very repetitive. Besides the decent story telling I thought everything else was very average. The environmental details are pretty basic and nothing really stands out. I'm not saying this is a bad game because it's not. I just feel there could have been much more they could have done to compliment the solid story they put in place.

developer_response.body:
Hi Edward, thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts on Journey to Foundation! We're glad to hear you enjoyed the story and the aspect of choices & consequences.

We really appreciate all your feedback. Thanks again for your review!

reviews.review_title: Great game but needs a DLC
reviews.review_description: I love this game but in my opinion it needs more to it like a DLC or more content added in it however the game has great story and cool characters it’s a good game to purchase

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks so much for your review of Journey to Foundation, and we're glad to hear you enjoyed it so much!

reviews.review_description:
I like it. It’s a shooter with puzzles mixed in. The story is fun and I like the idea of investigating things. I did drop a star because the game does not allow you to swap your movement controls. As a left handed person I should be able to run with my right hand and shoot with my left. Fix this and I’ll come back and add a star.

Edit: in reply to the dev comments, I’m asking you to make it so my right hand moves me forward, not my left. It’s difficult to move forward and aim with the same hand.

developer_response.body:
Hi there Stuweeee, thanks for taking the time to leave a review! We're glad to hear you're enjoying your adventure.

We're happy to share that you can change your dominant hand in the game settings.

Open your game settings using the Options button on the left controller, then select Settings in the game settings menu.

From here, you can change "Dominant Hand" to "Left" within the Calibration menu at the top (Comfort settings).

With the left-hand dominant setting, L3 will be crouch, R3 will be Mentalic Ping, and your gun holster will move to your left hip, allowing you to shoot with your left hand using the trigger button.

Movement controls will remain the same, with your left thumbstick propelling you forward, and your right thumbstick allowing you to rotate.

We hope this helps! Happy gaming.

reviews.review_description: Very slow movements, janky, characters take annoying pauses when they talk. Graphics not up to the task

developer_response.body:
Hi there. We're sorry to hear about your game experience. We'd love to investigate this for you further.

Please create a support ticket at support.journeytofoundation.com with additional information so we can investigate and ensure you have the intended experience!

We look forward to assisting you.

Warm Regards,
The Archiact Team

reviews.review_description:
This game made me angry because it could be very easy to make it perfect!

It needs 3 small changes: 
1) make npcs graphically more interesting!
They are all the same! There is only one npc that is different from the other ones;
2) allow to replay starting from key choices, without having to redo everything; 
3) make players a gift, when they finish the game! 
You can't simply write "The End"!
Reward the player and his choices with a final video (like Mass Effect 3 or The Witcher 3), which summarizes what will happen as a result of the decisions made. 

I finished the game making some choices, I tried to load the last checkpoint, but...I can do nothing different! I should start another run from zero! In this way there is no replayablity!

So now I give a 1; if something changes I will give more.

developer_response.body:
Hi Zack, thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts. We appreciate your feedback.

Our development teams including Art, Animation, and Narrative worked hard to create unique NPCs, differentiated by their clothing, personalities, values, alignment, and dialogue, so we're disappointed to hear you found them to be so similar.

At any point, you can reload your last checkpoint within the menu settings, which in some cases will allow you to redo certain conversations and choices. We also encourage multiple playthroughs so you can experience new choices and consequences!

Thanks again for your feedback. If you enjoyed your journey, we hope you'll update the rating to reflect your enjoyment of the game itself!

All the best,
The Archiact Team

reviews.review_description: The story was ok not to fun there was more dialogue then action or gameplay I did like the tattletales style but that is it

I liked the book and the series. I hoped to find something interesting and challenging,  but the excellent story is spoiled by the bad way it's told. Graphics are OK. Not great, not bad. Just OK. Puzzles are not challenging at all and the game play leaves literally no freedom at all. It really not worth the money I paid.

The game play is a bit underwhelming and most of the dialog choices don't seem to really affect anything because the game *wants* you to choose certain things in order for it to progress, so it will keep showing dialog options until you choose the "right" one. Infact its often possible to go through all the dialog options in one go. You'd think that choosing a particular option earlier would predispose your and other characters to give certain responses eg choosing an aggressive response earlier might cause further aggressive responses to appear later or be received from other characters but it doesn't seem to work like that, which is a shame. There are a few choices that are critical to the story and choosing incorrectly results in mission failure but overall I felt like most of the choices were unnecessary. This game definitely doesn't feel worth $40 but I paid significantly less and I always like sci-fi so I'm more or less happy. Also, the graphics in this game suck! I don't know what  they were thing but it was the wrong thing.

This games graphics aren’t all that to begin with but I still stuck it out because the storyline was intriguing. Your gun sucks throughout the ENTIRE storyline. There is no character customization, it’s glitchy after a while of playing, and you can’t run at all. Needless to say I wasted my money. So disappointed. I liked it at first and it had so much potential but it felt cheaply made. Not only that but the end of the game was stupid. It seemed like you’d just continue and level up your character but nope. Overall I’m very unhappy with this game.

I thought this game would be interesting and would keep me entertained during the holiday break, but is not be so. Controls are jerky and counter intuitive, so trying to move around is a pain. All you do walk around listening to silly people. Very boring and a waste of money. Will be returning the game.

What good is a 120Hz headset if you see clouds in the distance move with a framerate that is closer to 1 Hz (yes that is 1 frame per second). A totally immersion breaking experience.
And that while everything is terribly pixelated and not aliased at all. Is this game even running at full resolution ? To top it off, reflections in windows that look like they come straight out of a minecraft level. I find it all subpar for a full price game.

reviews.review_title: Distortion on the right side of quest 3
reviews.review_description: It feels like it would be an amazing game, however I just can't play it. There is a space image covering up the right hand side of the screen, and it feels like there is a permanent vignette. Will come back to it for the quest 3 patch.

developer_response.body:
Hi there John, thanks for sharing your experience with Journey to Foundation. We're sorry about the issue you were experiencing on the Meta Quest 3!

We're really happy to share that we've just released a Meta Quest 3 update that addresses the field of view issue for this platform, and includes some additional bug fixes and strong visual updates that we think will make your experience even better.

We hope you'll update the game and give it another shot!

All the best,
The Archiact Team

reviews.review_description:
bio,site/Jagged81
Replace , with .  On internet address.
So far awesome game..

The game is msde well, but the gameplay itself is near non-existent. The dialogue is good, but nit food enough to carry a $40 VR game for me, as the story never fully grabbed me in the 2 hours I apparently played..
(im unable to even get refunded, thus adding ti the offense...
MAKE THE REFUND POLICY LONGER THAN 2 HOURS FOR LONGER GAMES META!).

With the dialogue and story being 98% of anything this game offers, I just had to lose $40 I could've spent on something like Assassin's Creed Nexus.
If I want deep story and dialogue, I'll pay half the price and watch it in movie/series form. Not VR..

This game is great from what I've seen for people who want this story and do not mind playing a dialogue selection VR game with occasional busy work to break up conversating for a minute or two, at the price of a full TV season or actual video games.

On Quest 3:
I can forgive the slow storytelling, the bad, low textured graphics and even the unchallenging gameplay. But the game is verry glitchy: All text is cut off mid-word to te next line whitch annoys me a lot more than I thought it would. Converstion choices activate ramdomly. 

What finaly ruined it for me is:
When I started a convesation when I met Captain "What's het name"  for the seccond time somewhere else on The Array, I was teleported to the start of the corridor and was presented with the same conversation I had with her at the beginning of the game..

It just feels sloppy, unfonished and uninspired.

developer_response.body:
[Update December 22/23]
Hi there Dutch_Guy. We're sorry to hear you've run into these issues.

We just wanted to share that we've released a Meta Quest 3 update today along with some recent patches that have addressed the line-breaking issue you were experiencing.

We hope you'll update your game to see the visual and gameplay upgrades that are now out for Meta Quest 3, and hope you have a more enjoyable experience with these updates.

All the best,
The Archiact Team

reviews.review_title: Fairly good story, bad everything else.
reviews.review_description:
Journey to Foundation is not a good game. Combat is too simple, and this game does that thing so many other VR games do with their shooting: the bad guys never miss, ever. There is only one kind of puzzle and it wears out its welcome almost immediately. Linearity in a game isn’t necessarily a bad thing, but JtF is too linear. Levels are too simple. And dialogue choices don’t really matter. 

This game tells a fairly OK story, 3/5, but is straight up a 1/5 as a game. It just isn’t fun to play.

reviews.review_title: Strong on story but dull on gameplay
reviews.review_description:
🚨FULL REVIEW VIDEO AVAILABLE ON YOUTUBE BY SEARCHING ‘GAMERTAG VR JOURNEY TO FOUNDATION’ 🚨

Today we’re taking a look at Journey to foundation on PSVR2 & Meta Quest3 and after recently watching the tv series ‘foundation’ on Apple TV, I was pretty hyped to jump in but its been a mixed bag to play on both platforms and don’t get me wrong, the game is indeed what it always said it was, a game about exploration, choices and dynamic conversations and sometimes, it works really well but I also found myself struggling to concentrate and care a lot do the time, but lets first go over the games Story because unless you watched the tv show or read the foundation books by Issac Asimov, you might struggle.

 A man called Hari Seldon predicted mathematically that the current ruling force of the universe, the galactic empire will unavoidably fall and all of humanity will enter 10,000 years of darkness. Hari Seldon plans to shorten the dark years to only 1000 by making a place called foundation. 

Foundation is where all our knowledge, what’s best about humanity will be stored and preserved to help us get back on our feet quicker. That’s the short version but the way and I definitely recommend the tv show. 
So onto journey to foundation. 

You play an agent with the Commission of Public Safety that maintains order in the Galactic Empire. You have a gift, an ability to read peoples emotions and manipulate them during conversations. 

Your first mission is to investigate the possible kidnapping of the governors daughter which escalates into an undercover mission and has you making some pretty cool choices and the stakes get pretty huge with everything you get caught up in. 

As I said earlier as a fan of the show I enjoyed the dialogue and the story but I did lose interest more than a few times and I kept hoping things would pick up the pace but they never really do. You see Journey to foundation is roughly 60% talking, 20%picking cubes up to watch videos, listen to voice notes and read more dialogue then I would guess 15% puzzle solving and then only 5% action and unfortunately the action is pretty boring. The Ai moves around convincingly enough and the transforming gun which you collect different versions of is pretty cool, but these moments of combat are slow, uninspired and pretty boring.  It also fluctuates in presentation. Sometimes the environments can look good but they can also look pretty bad and slightly broken. The characters are the star of the show which is arguably the most important thing and lip sync was never a problem and they are expressive and well voice acting which is good because without that effort put into these characters Im not sure I could have kept playing. 

The puzzles which aren’t always puzzles more like something to do are repetitive and boring and serve as more a fillers or distractions . Pull this lever, join these dots, put the battery in there and only twice did I get really stuck and I have to mention one of them incase you play it and find the same issue

During the game you have timed gestures. If you don’t do the gesture in time then you fail or a different decision will be made. One puzzle was with a Lady who wanted me to bless her by doing certain gestures with my hands. The image was putting my thumb and finger together and even though I was certain I was doing it right and tried every different way she wouldn’t let me past and it kept reloading the scene which made it more frustrating, until after 40 mins she randomly said ok? And the other puzzle was actually my bad for not looking around enough due to frustration of the hand gesture issue that happened just before it. 

Anyway aside from those times overall you're pretty much just going through motions and overall I found it pretty boring. I knew the game was heavily focused on choices and conversations but I guess I want prepared for quite so much standing and talking. Before I summarise I played this both on PSVR2 and Meta Quest 3 but I’m pretty certain it was the quest 2 version. Quest 2 is a roller coaster of visuals and I would guess this was developed for PSVR2 and stripped back for Quest because characters aside the game has had some sacrifices made. It looks ok but now I’ve seen the power of Quest 3 with games like red matter 2, it makes this game look like a bad drawing in places. On PSVR2 I’m pretty certain it's using reprojected and looks good. There isn’t any wow moments but it looks ok. 

So to summarise be aware of what you're getting into. This is a talking game broken up with uninspired shooting parts and some not difficult puzzles. I did have a slighty good time but I also struggled to stay awake and kept resisting the urge to skip dialogue. The climbing and shooting are the only parts that changed the pace enough for me to keep going and it’s not a game I would personally revisit. I’ve played point and click games like monkey island and universe so I don’t need to have constant action and I love puzzle game story games but unfortunately this one didn’t win me over.

developer_response.body:
Hey Gamertag_VR, thanks for taking the time to give us your honest review of Journey to Foundation. We appreciate the feedback, and we're really happy to share that we have released a couple of patches that address user feedback, to improve the player experience (including making gestures register more easily, like you mentioned).

We've also just released a Meta Quest 3 update, which focuses on improving the graphics and visuals across the entire game for Meta Quest 3. When the game first launched, the game was playable on Meta Quest 3, but built for Meta Quest 2.

If you decide to give the game another shot with these updates in place, we hope you have a more enjoyable experience.

All the best,
The Archiact Team

reviews.review_description:
The shooting aim is wonky as all giddy up, but even accounting for that, I have to constantly reload checkpoints to get past bugs that kick in and lock me out of progressing/seeing the items I need to get past. This last bug has completely stopped gameplay, despite me reloading and even completely shutting off the game and then starting continuing again: confronting the viceroy on her ship orbiting terminus, when I died the first time it reloaded me to the point where I have to shoot the locks off her door, and I did that, but the doors no longer open to continue to the cutscene and subsequent battle. I'm completely blocked, pending a bug fix/patch. 

I would love to rate this game higher, because I feel without bugs the immersion and storytelling deserve it, but the bugs/halting gameplay is significantly disruptive. Please fix so I can (hopefully) amend my review later. I don't want to write it up as-is, because I feel with the right follow up it would deserve better.

developer_response.body:
[Update November 15/23]
Hi there, we're really sorry to hear you've ran into this issue! We recently released a patch that has addressed this. Once you update your game, you should be able to continue playing without issue.

Once again, we're deeply sorry about this disruption in your gameplay experience, and we hope you can continue enjoying your playthrough now!

reviews.review_description:
🍀Get 2 5 % Off !!!🍀

https : / / raketenpilot . bio . link

Great game :) Love it

This in my opinion is the 1st vr title that feels like you are playing a AAA title! the character designs and animations are up there with Dishonored and bioshock. the game play is much like mass effect where you question characters in order to understand what’s going on and to progress in the story. The gun play is small segments that breaks away from the monologue driven story telling to give you a sense of action. The puzzle solving doesn’t take too long to figure out. Like unlocking doors that you have to hack via security hacking with a holographic connect the circuit puzzle and so on. overall it’s a nice little masterpiece that branches away from all the other unimpressive vr titles. I’d say this game is very impressive! if you buy this game I’d go into it with the expectation of something campy and mind numbing fun.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts on Journey to Foundation!

The team worked really hard on bringing these characters to life and making beautiful spacescapes, so we're really happy to hear how much you're enjoying these aspects of the game.

Thank you again for your review, and we hope you continue to enjoy your experience with this immersive narrative adventure!

reviews.review_description: You are the main character in a sci-fi drama. After establishing the setting, the narrative unfolds in a dialogue tree. There are multiple competing factions, you must choose, and your choices matter. It's ambitious and I'm glad VR developers are trying different types of experiences. It is held back by some underwhelming graphics and token game-like sequences.

Final edit. Just finished it and it was a really good story and fun campaign. Unfortunately, I heard this Developer took a little bit of a hit so don’t know if there will be a sequel which is a shame because after the game was fixed, I really did enjoy my time playing it. combat could’ve been a little bit better, but the story definitely makes up for it. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️



EDIT: the developers did listen. They released an update pretty quick I originally refunded the game. I purchased it again because other than the bugs I was experiencing it seems like a very good game. I will post another update once I have Completed the game with a more detailed review review. 

I just finished genotype and left a review in the review I said I don’t normally write reviews which is true. But that game deserves recognition. Here I am not even a week later leaving another review but because of frustration. 25 minutes into the game and it’s already glitching and has a got me stuck in some strange Loop. I cannot speak to the person with the a icon instead it throws me all the way back across the room to someone I’ve previously talk to and I’m stuck in that same loop where he’s just repeating the same information. I try again and it throws me back across the room. This might be a good game and it seems like something that I would really like. But if I’m paying $40 I expect it to work. It really is unacceptable how they just release games and then decide they’ll fix the problems later. It’s like being punished for buying a game on release. Honestly, the fact that some of these bigger titles aren’t released with a and M3 update is a little frustrating but that’s forgivable not being able to play the game is not. Hopefully they’ll let me get my money back. I’ll probably come back and try it in a couple months after updates have been released.

developer_response.body:
Hi Steff, thank you for taking the time to update your review as you played through the game after some of our patches and updates.

We're really happy to hear how much you enjoyed your journey once you jumped back in, and we appreciate your feedback! Our team worked hard on creating a captivating narrative in an immersive universe. Thanks again for your review, and happy gaming!

reviews.review_description:
I killed someone because he wouldn't keep my secret. 10/10

Jokes aside, I like the how interactive and immersive this game is.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking time to leave a review! We're glad you're enjoying it, and the choices (and consequences) that you're faced with along your journey.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely mind blowing, but we NEED a sequel

developer_response.body: We're so glad to hear how much you enjoyed Journey to Foundation! Thank you for taking the time to leave a review, and we'll be sure to share if a sequel is coming.

reviews.review_description: This game in every sense is official must try and u make the decision to see where the game takes u 😁I love this game

developer_response.body: Hey Quelbin, thanks so much for sharing your thoughts on Journey to Foundation! We're so glad to hear how much you enjoyed it and all the decisions that came along with the story. We hope you'll try another playthrough with different choices! 😊

reviews.review_description: The voice acting is top notch the metallic abilities is pretty cool the gun modifiers are very interesting I think the story is good and I have never read the book version all the characters are good fleshed out characters but the environments are not blowing anyone away but it is still a 10/10 game

reviews.review_title: Like being a character in sci-fi tv
reviews.review_description: I love sci-fi tv ( including the Apple TV show Foundation) so this is so much fun for me - I feel as though I’m living in one of those shows ! I’m about 6 hours in and it’s gorgeous and exciting. Love the fact it’s story driven though all enjoy a bit of shooter action! The teleport option stops me from getting motion sick . Bravo to the developers!

developer_response.body:
Hi Jodie, thanks so much for your review of Journey to Foundation! We really wanted players to feel like they were the star of the show while playing, so we're thrilled to hear your experience.

We're happy to hear you've adjusted your movement settings to help you continue playing! Enjoy the rest of your journey.

reviews.review_title: Very good game! Message me for code.
reviews.review_description:
This is a story (book) driven interactive game. Isaak Asimov fans, it’s your day! About 6 hours of play total. 
If you like Red Matter or Hubris or Peaky Blinders, you will like this game. The puzzles are fun, there is some shooting, but it’s the story that will win you over. Sci-fi fans, this game is for you.

developer_response.body: Hi, French Fries! Thanks for taking the time to review Journey to Foundation. We're glad to hear you enjoyed the puzzles and story, and appreciate your kind words and recommendations to other gamers and sci-fi fans!

reviews.review_description:
So far the gunplay is “Hubris” mid and very janky but since its more like a movie where you play the protagonists i can look pass that if the story is good and so far i can say… i didn’t asked for a refund and the game is 45$can so there’s that

I need to play more but as a sci fi tale it seems interesting

Edit: its seems more janky and less shooter focus than i was expecting but the story, powers, moral choices, vr mechanics, etc are all very ambitious for a stand alone game from a scifi book serie. I can appreciate the level of complexity needed to tell a story on that kind of platform and will have a lot of fun going through that space adventure since i am a big Asimov fan for more than 30years

Re-edit:I find myself liking the system of weapon choices/switching... it make the shooting a bit more enjoyable mixed with the powers and weird setting, I can confirm, i like that little game

developer_response.body:
Hi Raz-R, thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts and experience with the game, and continue to update as you play more. We're glad to hear you're a fan of the ADM weapon system, Mentalics, and sci-fi setting of this narrative-driven adventure.

We sincerely appreciate the honest review and kind words you've shared about the different aspects of the game. We hope you continue to enjoy your experience!

reviews.review_description:
Update 2:
This is the only story based game that I’m interesting to play for a second round and actually plan for a 3rd one.😁It’s really nice to explore decisions impacts, plus overall balance and some games features makes such replays really enjoyable!
I just saw your response about upcoming Q3 update, that’s really awesome news!!🔥

Update 1:
I’m in chapter 5 now and what should I say. The game is even more great than I thought initially.👍 It’s the best story based sci-fi adventure available on platform, authors made really great job to implement plot and deliver idea. And I should admit really good gameplay balance, it has just enough of everything!

Original:
This is an awesome delivery of my favourite novel to VR!
The main plot, as well as the various ideas and concepts regarding the mental ability to control emotions, are presented brilliantly! Authors seemed spent really a lot of time to think and rethink to make it just right!

For those who are familiar with the Foundation novels, this game will already be a real find! And for those who aren't familiar but enjoy truly solid story-based adventures, this will be a treasure too!

It would be really nice to have some Q3 specific update with various enhancements. 
The story flow picks up and drives absolutely great and it’s a long and great adventure!

My big thanks to the team!⚡️

developer_response.body:
[Update December 22/23]
Hi there igenvector, thank you for your honest review; we're glad to hear your enjoying the game!

We're excited to share that the Meta Quest 3 update is now available! We've squashed bugs, made improvements to the gameplay based on user feedback, and the Meta Quest 3 update comes with some great visual upgrades.

We're glad to hear you've enjoyed multiple playthroughs! We hope you'll do another one with the new Quest 3 update. :)

reviews.review_description: I haven't gotten far yet still at the beginning but I like it so far but one point got me annoyed there was a choice to be made and the out come was not clear other then that great

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts so far on Journey to Foundation! We're glad to hear you're liking it. If you'd like to try out a different choice, you can always try reloading your last checkpoint and trying a different conversation option.

Hope this helps! We hope you enjoy the rest of your journey. :)

reviews.review_description: Currently 6 and a Half hours into the game and despite a couple of minor glitches the game has run incredibly smoothly, the gameplay is slow-paced, dialogue heavy, but that doesn't make it a bad thing from my point of view, in fact it enhances my enjoyment of the game, as the story slowly unfolds and the characters display their different emotions, given the various situations. When the combat kicks in it is both intense and excellently handled (I'm not great at shooting games, but am enjoying the combat sections very much), if you enjoyed games like Hubris, then you will absolutely love Journey to Foundation, there is no requirement to have read the books, but the influences are there all around you, at times it's almost like being a character in a book, it's that engrossing... I've played some top notch standalone games this year, but this one is rapidly heading to the top of my personal pile, for best Quest game of the year... Once the Quest 3 enhanced graphic version is released I will definitely replay this game, although I'll be making differing choices in my next playthrough, as the potential for changing the outcome is for me very intriguing. Anybody that has any doubts about the game can check out my episodic playthrough on my YouTube channel, which  starts dropping on the 11th November (I won't put links here, I'm pretty easy to find on YT for anyone that's interested).

developer_response.body:
Hi Max, thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts! We're really glad to hear how much you're enjoying the story and combat, and that you've found yourself to feel so part of the game - that's what we were aiming for.

We're excited to share that we've got a Quest 3 update in the works, and we'll be sure to let you know once we have more information on its release!

reviews.review_description: I like it a lot love the story , weapons hope you guys do part 2 I love everything Foundation

developer_response.body: Hi Space Wizard, thanks so much for taking the time to review Journey to Foundation! We're really glad to hear you're enjoying the game and are a big fan of the IP, and happy to see you'd like a part 2! :)

reviews.review_description:
"Journey to Foundation" is an absolute masterpiece in the world of VR gaming! This game immerses you in a stunning, futuristic universe like no other. The graphics are mind-blowing and the attention to detail is awe-inspiring. The storyline is gripping, and the gameplay mechanics are smooth and intuitive I found myself completely engrossed in the captivating narrative and the challenges it presented. The game's audio design enhances the overall experience, making it truly unforgettable. It's a breathtaking journey that combines adventure, strategy, and exploration in a way that only VR can deliver. "Journey to Foundation" is a must-play for any VR enthusiast, earning it a solid five stars!

Ps, The only thing that disappointed me is the length, while it is very long for a VR game I found out that it if you wanted to you could finish the game in 5 to 6 hours.

developer_response.body:
Hey Bio, thank you for your review! We really appreciate the kind words and are thrilled to hear how much you enjoyed it. We put a lot of thought and careful curation into all aspects of the game, including the audio, so it's great to hear how much you enjoyed those pieces.

We hope you'll do another playthrough of the game and make some different choices to experience things differently!

reviews.review_title: oh god its great. give it time
reviews.review_description: This game scratches an itch for me that hasn't been scratched since i finished mass effect. its not perfect by any means, and does itself no favours by delaying getting into combat as long as it does, nor by the first weapon having no reticule. makes use of a lot of great vr ideas like hacking and cutting torch ,  etc. where it shines is once you unlock the better weapons , powers and from the start of the game to finish the dialogue system. well worth your time. The game also wears its mass effect heart on its sleeve, with a main character named Ward (i see what you did there) and conversation ending lines like ' i should go'.  i know they had a bunch of layoffs recently but god i hope this gets a sequel or ue5 graphical overhall for pcvr. if i could change anything in this game it would be to front load it with a previous mission that sets things in motion that gets you into action early as was case with mass effect to pull people who are more actiony in from the get go.

developer_response.body:
Hi there James! Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts on Journey to Foundation. We're thrilled to hear how much you're enjoying your adventure!

We appreciate the feedback, and enjoyed reading about the pieces of the game that really stood out to you. :)
